title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
Hercule Poirot and his companion, Carstairs, debate the details of the three murders involving Harriet Sipper, Ada Gransbury, and Richard Nigges. While Carstairs focuses on the similarity of the cufflinks placed in the victims' mouths, Poirot emphasizes the importance of the discrepancies. Specifically, Poirot notes that the window in Nigges's room was left open, whereas the windows in the other two crime scenes were closed. He argues that because the killer went to such lengths to arrange the bodies in identical positions, the open window is a significant inconsistency.

Carstairs proposes a theory that the killer moved through rooms 317, 238, and 121 to facilitate a quick escape, but Poirot argues this timeline is impossible given the narrow window between the delivery of poisoned drinks at 7:15 and the discovery of the bodies. Poirot also notes the audacity of the killer, who left a note on the front desk stating, "May they rest in peace, 121, 238, 317," which was found by hotel employee John Good at 8:20.

The hotel manager, Lazari, joins the discussion and defends the moral character of the hotel guests. Poirot orders that everyone present at the hotel on Thursday be gathered at Mr. Kitchbull's place to provide their whereabouts. He instructs Carstairs to investigate the victims' residences, the employees who booked their rooms, and the delivery details of their food and drinks. Poirot also asks to check for a woman named Jeanne, though Lazari claims no such person works at the Bluxton Hotel. Later, Poirot secures Room 238 alone to investigate whether the killer escaped through the window.
